title: Browser artifact loader — runtime fetch-manifest orchestration in host-side Rust: injected fetch, deps-first ordering with SCC tolerance, ArtifactLoadError, additive report vocabulary, zero wake-path call sites
purpose:
The artifact-loader pillar governed by the WPORT-6 section of `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:125-136` at `03a8474`. The arc's SCOPE, verbatim: "Add runtime fetch/manifest orchestration over the existing `load_module(bytes)` primitive. The loader orders dependencies, reports fetch and decoding failures, and preserves unresolved-import reporting. This is runtime browser loading, not another build-time inline bundle path." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:130` at `03a8474`). The arc's ACCEPTANCE SHAPE, verbatim: "A browser test starts with a manifest URL, fetches real artifacts at runtime, loads dependencies before dependants, and executes a module through the real VM. Missing artifacts, malformed bytes, cycles or unsatisfied ordering, and unresolved imports produce structured failures naming the affected artifact/module. No progress depends on recurring checks; fetch completion re-enters through the event-driven scheduling contract." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:134` at `03a8474`). The arc's frozen dependency line is WPORT-2 (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:128` at `03a8474`); see the DEPENDENCY NOTE in the task.

DESIGN STANCE (decided at the outline, drafted here as decided text): the loader is HOST-SIDE RUST IN BEAMR-WASM — a new exported async surface on `WasmVm` (own file pair under the 500-line law) that takes a manifest URL plus an INJECTED fetch capability (a JS `Function` returning `Promise<bytes>`), fetches and parses a NEW versioned runtime fetch manifest, orders modules dependencies-first by the manifest's explicit dependency edges (deterministic order inside cycles), loads each artifact through the in-crate core loader (`beamr::loader::load_module_with_origin`) so `LoadError` variants are matched in-crate rather than re-parsed from flattened strings, and returns a JSON-string batch report that PRESERVES today's truly-unresolved vocabulary and ADDITIVELY surfaces the deferred + denied buckets core already computes. Operational failures reject the returned Promise with one named error class (`ArtifactLoadError`, the `ConnectionEventProtocolError` pattern) carrying kind slugs. Zero wake-path call sites, zero polling, zero changes to any existing export, build-time bundle untouched.

WHY NOT BYTECODE ORCHESTRATION — THE RECORDED REASON (cited, not asserted; ground pack §8.2): bytecode-orchestrated loading is broken as the bytes stand. A process suspended on an async-NIF Promise (the fetch shape) leaves the VM SETTLED IDLE — `settled_idle = true_idle && !has_pending_deadline()` covers only native+receive deadlines (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:829-836`, doc comment `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:941-946`; true_idle `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:753-762`), `has_pending_work`'s doc says an in-flight Promise does not count (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:765-778`), and the WPORT-4 test `promise_completion_from_true_idle_queues_one_turn_with_durable_result` asserts true-idle with two in-flight "fetch" Promises (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:3242-3277`). Consequently `await_exit(pid)` on a loader process suspended in a fetch NIF resolves 'idle' immediately, and any settling drain flushes ALL waiters 'idle' (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:818-826`) — awaitExit cannot await a bytecode-orchestrated multi-fetch load. Fixing that would need a wrapper-visible outstanding-completion count that does not exist (spawn_local continuations are fire-and-forget, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1263-1298`) — a core redesign WPORT-6 does not need. WHY NOT PURE HOST JS: it puts structured failures and ordering logic in untyped script — the edge-worker stub-drift precedent (completion/summary shapes duplicated untype-checked in `examples/edge-worker/test/worker.test.mjs`, ground pack §8.6, hazard 28). Rust-in-beamr-wasm it is.

PIN DISCIPLINE, binding: evidence pin `03a8474`. The evidence ground is `wport6-ground-pack.md` (checker-corrected synthesis of seven tracer reports; its §10 disagreement table is authoritative over any tracer claim). Every code citation in this brief is byte-identical between `8922e2a` and `03a8474` (`git diff --stat 8922e2a 03a8474` touches only `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md`, +3/−1); arc-doc citations use `03a8474` line numbers throughout (the pack's 8922e2a arc numbers shift +2 for lines >= ~111). The build worker MUST re-verify the pin-shift set named in the verification block at its ACTUAL base commit before writing a line — believed state is not citable state.
task:
EVIDENCE PIN `03a8474`, stated as binding: the ground for every claim below is `wport6-ground-pack.md` (same artifacts directory as this draft), evidence pin `8922e2a` with tree `03a8474`, sole delta the arc doc — all code citations byte-identical at both commits, arc citations given here at `03a8474`. Fourteen tear questions were carried to Artemis's outline; ten are DECIDED below (D1-D14, numbered by the pack's §11 TQ numbers), four are carved as OQ-A..OQ-D in this draft's open_questions with recommendations attached.

D1 MANIFEST — fresh format, decided. A new versioned runtime fetch manifest: `{"format":"beamr-fetch-manifest","version":1,"modules":[{"name":<module name>,"url":<artifact URL>,"deps":[<names>]}]}`. URLs resolve relative to the manifest URL. The build-time `beamr-wasm-bundle` manifest is NOT extended — nothing in the repo consumes it (`crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs:126-151` hand-builds it; zero code consumers repo-wide, ground pack §2) — and extending it would couple the must-not-duplicate build path to runtime semantics. Byte representation: PER-MODULE `.beam` fetches; `modules.bin` is explicitly NOT a fetch target (linear-scan `EmbeddedArchive::get`, `crates/beamr/src/loader/embed.rs:197-202`, plus the double-representation hazard — pack hazards 19/21). The manifest is independently authored (or emitted by future tooling — out of scope); `BEAMR_WASM_BUNDLE_DIR` never enters the runtime path. This decides arc open question 4 (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:213` at `03a8474`): a versioned runtime manifest, not a standardization of the generated one. OQ-A (reserved `integrity` field) is carved.

D2 ORDERING — topo-with-SCC-tolerance plus post-batch verification, decided. Kahn order over declared deps; deps-before-dependants satisfied for the acyclic part (the arc acceptance verbatim). Within a strongly-connected component, members load in manifest order — a dependency cycle is NOT a load failure (mutual recursion is legal BEAM reality; Deferred imports heal at call time: `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/core.rs:561-599`, test `call_ext_deferred_target_resolves_when_module_loads_later` at `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/tests.rs:1867`) but IS a structured, named report entry listing the SCC members — this satisfies the arc's "cycles ... produce structured failures naming the affected artifact/module" clause as a structured DIAGNOSIS. FATAL structured failures: a dep edge naming a module absent from the manifest (`dependency_missing` — this is the arc's "unsatisfied ordering"), duplicate module names (`manifest_malformed`), missing artifact (fetch failure), malformed bytes. After the batch, a POST-BATCH VERIFICATION PASS walks each module's report: any deferred entry whose target is IN the manifest set must be verified healed — the observable is a registry/export lookup (or a call through the import) succeeding for the target, never mutated bucket state: `resolved_imports` is immutable inside the `Arc<Module>` and healing is call-time re-resolution (pack §3); any deferred entry whose target is ABSENT is reported as `missing_dependencies` data; gate3/unregistered targets are reported as data, never failure (gate3 is not registered on the browser — real artifacts routinely report non-empty unresolved at load, pack §7, hazard 11). OQ-B (cycle fatality) is carved; the flip costs one branch and one wall.

D3 LOCUS — Rust in beamr-wasm, decided; bytecode orchestration RULED OUT (settled-idle evidence in the purpose block, cited); pure host JS RULED OUT (stub-drift precedent, hazard 28). Surface shape: `pub fn load_artifacts(&self, manifest_url: String, fetch: js_sys::Function) -> js_sys::Promise` via `wasm_bindgen_futures::future_to_promise` (wasm-bindgen-futures 0.4.73 is already a production dep, `crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:26`; `JsFuture`/`spawn_local` already used at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1267-1268`). `&self` ingress per the connection-events hub precedent (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:348-349`). Shared-handle ground [re-verified at 03a8474]: every `WasmVm` field is a clonable shared handle (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:50-62` — `atom_table: Arc<AtomTable>`, `module_registry: Arc<ModuleRegistry>`, `bif_registry: Arc<BifRegistryImpl>`, `scheduler: Rc<RefCell<WasmScheduler>>`, the rest `Rc`), and the core load call consumes exactly the three `Arc`s plus an origin (pack §1) — so the loader future holds `Arc` clones and NEVER touches the scheduler `RefCell`. Discipline, grep-provable: no RefCell borrow held across an await point; in this brief that is strengthened to ZERO `.borrow` calls in `artifact_loader.rs` (each fetch resolves in its own microtask continuation; the synchronous load step needs no borrow at all). A discovered need to borrow the scheduler inside the loader is a spec-underdetermined STOP, not an improvisation. Re-entrancy: calling `load_artifacts` from inside an in-drain callback is the same documented no as everything else (hazard 16); the loader itself runs in its own microtask turns, never inside a drain.

D4 FETCH ACQUISITION — explicit injection only, decided. A JS `Function` taking a URL string, returning `Promise<ArrayBuffer|Uint8Array>`. No web-sys (dep tree untouched — `crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:13-36` has none, repo-wide grep zero hits), no Reflect-probe of `globalThis.fetch` (explicitness over magic; the browser probe passes a one-line adapter and the probe doc records it). Non-Promise or wrong-typed resolution rejects with the `fetch_protocol` kind slug. RULING RESPECTED BY CONSTRUCTION, stated so the tear does not re-litigate it: the port-design doc's ruling that browser fetch rides `WasmAsyncNifFacility`, "not onto beamr's io module" (`docs/WASM-RUNTIME-PORT-DESIGN.md:158-161`) governs BYTECODE-VISIBLE fetch (guest BIFs); the loader is host-side orchestration and exposes nothing to bytecode.

D5 WAKE-PATH BAR — moot by construction, interpretation recorded. Loading registers code and produces no runnable edge (`WasmVm::load_module` ends at `json_to_js`, makes NO scheduling call — pack §1); the loader makes ZERO scheduling calls. Execution after load is the host's explicit act via existing exports (`spawn` / `send_message` / `spawn_actor`). The bar counts call sites; the loader adds none — the WPORT-4/5 prohibition text is inherited verbatim in the LAWS BLOCK below, and the pack §4 call-site inventory is quoted in R2 as the closed set the final diff must not extend.

D6 FAILURE VOCABULARY — one class + kind slugs + data report, decided. One named class `ArtifactLoadError` (`js_sys::Error`, `set_name`, `"{kind}: {detail}"` message — byte-follows the `ConnectionEventProtocolError` pattern at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/connection_events.rs:515-519`, the repo's only named-class precedent). Kind slugs (closed set, pinned by test): `manifest_fetch_failed`, `manifest_malformed`, `dependency_missing`, `artifact_fetch_failed`, `fetch_protocol`, `artifact_invalid_format`, `artifact_decode_failed`, `artifact_validation_failed` — the last three mirror `LoadError` variants matched IN-CRATE (`crates/beamr/src/error.rs:16-30`; reachable set from malformed bytes is `InvalidFormat`/`MissingChunk`/`DecodeError`/`ValidationError`, pack §1 — `MissingChunk` maps to `artifact_invalid_format`), which is where hazard 2 (variant erasure at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:163`) dies. Batch semantics: FAIL-FAST; the rejection carries the failing artifact name/url, the stage, and the names already loaded before the failure — honest about the no-unload reality (the code_management facility is deliberately absent on wasm: `WasmScheduler::native_services` at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:792-811` injects no code_management_facility, pack §7). Payload mechanics are OQ-D (carved, drafter's pick attached). Per-module reporting on SUCCESS is data in the resolved JSON string (house convention, `json_to_js` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1588-1590`, hazard 5), schema pinned in R1. Existing `WasmVm::load_module` and its flat-string error are UNTOUCHED (additive-only boundary; retrofitting is out of scope).

D7 UNRESOLVED VOCABULARY — preserve + additively surface, decided. The truly-unresolved array keeps today's exact entry shape (`{"module","function","arity"}` per `unresolved_imports_to_json`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1538-1558`). Deferred + denied ride as NEW sibling arrays — the core accessors exist at `crates/beamr/src/loader/load.rs:183-190` (`deferred_imports`) and `crates/beamr/src/loader/load.rs:203-210` (`denied_imports`), unexported to JS today; the loader is in-crate and reads them directly — ZERO core change. "Resolvable later" vs "never registerable" is answered by the post-batch pass (D2), not by new core state. Healing a permanent Unresolved without reloading the dependant is explicitly OUT (nothing in the bytes supports it — `resolved_imports` is immutable inside `Arc<Module>`, pack §3; hazard 7's reload note lands in FETCH-MANIFEST.md's hazards section, not in scope).

D8 MODULE ORIGIN — carved as OQ-C, recommendation IN (additive `ModuleOrigin::Fetched`). See open_questions; R1 carries the contingent file set.

D9 IDEMPOTENCY — per-batch dedupe only, decided. Duplicate names within one manifest = `manifest_malformed` (fatal). Across calls: clobber-with-generation+1 is the documented reload semantic (`ModuleRegistry::insert_version`, `crates/beamr/src/module.rs:280-304` — re-load always succeeds, prior old version silently discarded, pack §1); the loader neither guards nor dedupes across batches. Hot-reload via re-fetch is thereby a FEATURE, and the export-dropped-on-reload poisoning order (hazard 7: stale-dep-loaded, dependant-loaded, dep-upgraded bakes permanent Undef into the dependant, `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/core.rs:600-608`) is a documented hazard line in FETCH-MANIFEST.md, not machinery.

D10 FIXTURES — own fixture dir, decided. `crates/beamr-wasm/fixtures/` with committed `.beam` next to `.erl` sources (house convention from `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures/`), included via `include_bytes!` from the wasm test module. Small closed set: a dep chain (a needs b needs c), a mutual cycle pair, a module with an unresolved import, one malformed-bytes blob (crafted, with a comment naming which `LoadError` it must produce). NO cross-crate `include_bytes!` into `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures` (packaging fragility). FIRST-NESS IS A STATED DELIVERABLE: these are the repo's first wasm-side executions of the real `.beam` decode path and the first real-VM JS-orchestration tests — at `03a8474` no wasm test exercises `load_module` and the decoder is bypassed entirely on wasm (pack §6).

D11 CI ARITHMETIC — inherited law. New wasm tests extend the exact-name list AND the exact-count summary in the SAME COMMIT, delta-states-cause in the commit message (the WPORT-5 OQ7 pattern). Loader tests live in their own pinned file (`artifact_loader_tests.rs`, joining the battery as `connection_events_tests.rs` did). The completion/summary vocabulary is NOT extended (the loader has its own report shape), so the edge-worker stubs stay untouched — hazard 28 avoided.

D12 BROWSER ACCEPTANCE — probe pattern, decided. Node CI walls with the injected fetch double resolving fixture bytes (the only hermetic shape — Node's undici fetch rejects file:// URLs and CI serves no HTTP, pack §6), plus a manual browser probe artifact `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-6-PROBE-FETCH.md` authored as a deliverable of this brief and run POST-LAND on Tom's/Annabel's word: real `fetch()`, real HTTP server, real manifest URL — observations NEVER CI acceptance (the WPORT-3 probe precedent verbatim, `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-3-PROBE-THROTTLE.md:1-28`). Ops note: the probe session can share a browser sitting with the still-OPEN WPORT-3 deadline probe; sequencing stays unowned by this brief.

D13 PID-COLLISION RIDER — CARRIED as an R3 line item (MUST). Waffles' reduced disposition, quoted verbatim from the arc at `03a8474` (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:113` — the ruling exists ONLY at `03a8474`, one commit past the pack's code pin): "(3) The pid-collision boarded candidate is REDUCED to a collision-pin regression test riding the next wasm brief as a line item (see the WPORT-4 board-finding supersession note): the fix is real — the private counter is deleted and `alloc_pid` on the shared mint is the sole allocator at `1ec8ec9` — but unpinned; the wall proves native and cooperative spawns mint distinct pids." WPORT-6 is the next wasm brief. Code ground: `alloc_pid` at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:702` is the sole allocator, called from both spawn sites `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:319` and `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:361`. The wall proves native-path and cooperative-path spawns mint distinct pids; it joins the pinned battery and the CI count.

D14 CODE:ALL_LOADED — OUT, decided. The facility stays un-wired; badarg-refusal stands per the WPORT-5 "registration must not imply support" discipline. Wiring it flips six sealed profile rows' documented classes for zero loader value — the loader needs no BIF to see the registry (it is in-crate). Named boundary below.

LAWS BLOCK (verbatim carriers): NO-POLLING (arc binding text); WPORT-4/5 wake-path prohibition (zero new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call sites outside tests); pull-before-start/push-when-landed; fresh-lint law; CI exact-name+exact-count same-commit; believed-state-is-not-citable-state (every pack citation re-verified at the build base); Vesper's encode/** claim; no builds in temp dirs; gate bar 9 legs on the final head. The NO-POLLING binding text, verbatim from the arc: "NO-POLLING (Tom's ruling): event-loop-driven scheduling — run-to-idle, wake on message; a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION (F-0d acceptance). Any timer whose job is “check whether something changed” is a design error." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:21` at `03a8474`).

STOP CONDITIONS, binding on the build worker: (1) anywhere this spec is underdetermined at the bytes, STOP and return with alternatives — never improvise a contract; (2) any APPARENT need for a new wake-path call site (`request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` outside tests) is a STOP, not a workaround — the loader's design premise is that it needs none; (3) any cascade forcing a change to existing export behavior (signatures, return shapes, error strings, completion/summary vocabulary) is a STOP — the additive-only boundary is load-bearing.

DEPENDENCY NOTE: the arc's frozen dependency line is WPORT-2 (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:128` at `03a8474`). This brief declares `depends_on: WPORT-5` — truthful and stronger, per the WPORT-5 precedent: WPORT-5 landed first (CLOSED at `1ec8ec9`, arc `:113` at `03a8474`), its land carries the `alloc_pid` sole-allocator state this brief's D13 wall pins, and its 33-name CI baseline is the arithmetic this brief extends. WPORT-2 through WPORT-4 are landed and subsumed by that edge.

requirements.title: Manifest + loader core — FETCH-MANIFEST.md, `load_artifacts` on WasmVm, Kahn ordering with SCC tolerance and post-batch verification, ArtifactLoadError, preserved-plus-additive report vocabulary
requirements.spec:
NEW FILE PAIR under the 500-line law (WPORT-4 precedent: new wrapper subsystem = dedicated file pair, never lib.rs additions — `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs` production region is already ~1,595 lines, pack §8.5): `crates/beamr-wasm/src/artifact_loader.rs` + `crates/beamr-wasm/src/artifact_loader_tests.rs`, module registration in lib.rs only.

MANIFEST (D1): document the schema in a new `docs/design/beamr/FETCH-MANIFEST.md` — format table, a worked JSON example, the reserved `integrity` field (per the OQ-A ruling; RECOMMEND reserve-unenforced), and explicit NON-GOALS: no build-time coupling (the `beamr-wasm-bundle` manifest of `crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs:126-151` is not extended, `BEAMR_WASM_BUNDLE_DIR` never enters the runtime path), `modules.bin` is not a fetch target (hazards 19/21), and the hazard-7 reload note (replacing a dependency whose export set changed requires reloading dependants — documented, not mechanized). Schema v1: `{"format":"beamr-fetch-manifest","version":1,"modules":[{"name":<module name>,"url":<relative-or-absolute artifact URL>,"deps":[<module names>]}]}`; URLs resolve relative to the manifest URL; per-module `.beam` fetches.

EXPORTED SURFACE (D3): `pub fn load_artifacts(&self, manifest_url: String, fetch: js_sys::Function) -> js_sys::Promise` on `WasmVm`, built with `wasm_bindgen_futures::future_to_promise` (production dep already, `crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:26`). `&self` ingress (hub precedent, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:348-349`). The future holds `Arc` clones of `atom_table`/`module_registry`/`bif_registry` ([re-verified at 03a8474]: `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:50-62`) and calls `beamr::loader::load_module_with_origin` directly for each artifact, matching `LoadError` variants in-crate (hazard 2). BORROW DISCIPLINE, grep-provable: zero `.borrow` calls in `artifact_loader.rs` — the load path needs no scheduler access; each fetch resolves in its own microtask continuation. A discovered need to borrow the scheduler in the loader is a STOP.

FETCH INJECTION CONTRACT (D4): the injected `fetch` is called with one URL-string argument per artifact and per the manifest fetch; it must return a thenable resolving to `ArrayBuffer` or `Uint8Array`. A non-Promise return or wrong-typed resolution rejects the batch with `fetch_protocol`. No web-sys, no Reflect-probe of `globalThis.fetch`.

ORDERING (D2): Kahn's algorithm over the manifest's declared dep edges; deps load before dependants for the acyclic part. SCC members load in manifest order; each SCC is reported in the success report's `cycles` array (per the OQ-B ruling; RECOMMEND non-fatal). Fatal, pre-fetch: a dep edge naming a module absent from the manifest (`dependency_missing`), duplicate module names (`manifest_malformed`). Fatal, in-batch: artifact fetch failure (`artifact_fetch_failed`), malformed bytes (the three decode-stage slugs). POST-BATCH VERIFICATION: walk each loaded module's report — every deferred entry whose target is in the manifest set MUST be verified healed — the observable is registry presence + export lookup succeeding for the target (or a call through the import), never bucket state, since `resolved_imports` is immutable inside `Arc<Module>` and healing is call-time re-resolution (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/core.rs:561-599`); deferred entries with absent targets land in `missing_dependencies` data; gate3/unregistered targets are data, never failure (hazard 11).

DEDUPE (D9): per-batch only — duplicate names in one manifest are `manifest_malformed`; across calls the documented clobber-with-generation+1 reload semantic stands (`crates/beamr/src/module.rs:280-304`), no cross-batch guard.

ERROR CLASS (D6): `ArtifactLoadError` — `js_sys::Error` with `set_name("ArtifactLoadError")` and message `"{kind}: {detail}"`, byte-following `protocol_error()` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/connection_events.rs:515-519`. Closed kind-slug set, pinned by the R3 wall-13 enumeration test: `manifest_fetch_failed`, `manifest_malformed`, `dependency_missing`, `artifact_fetch_failed`, `fetch_protocol`, `artifact_invalid_format` (LoadError::InvalidFormat and MissingChunk), `artifact_decode_failed` (LoadError::DecodeError), `artifact_validation_failed` (LoadError::ValidationError). Fail-fast; the rejection carries the failing artifact name/url, the stage, and the names already loaded before the failure, via the OQ-D mechanics (drafter's pick: one `data` property on the Error holding a JSON string — see open_questions; tear ratifies). Loader failures ride the returned Promise's rejection ONLY — never the arbiter `fail()` latch (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:727-730`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:838-844`; pack §8.3).

SUCCESS REPORT (D7), pinned schema, JSON string per the house convention (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1588-1590`): `{"ok":true,"loaded":[{"module":<name>,"unresolved":[{"module","function","arity"},...],"deferred":[...],"denied":[...]}],"cycles":[[<SCC members>],...],"missing_dependencies":[{"module","function","arity"},...]}`. The `unresolved` entries preserve today's exact shape byte-for-byte (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1538-1558`); `deferred`/`denied` are ADDITIVE siblings read via the in-crate accessors `crates/beamr/src/loader/load.rs:183-190` and `crates/beamr/src/loader/load.rs:203-210` — zero core change for the report.

MODULE ORIGIN (contingent on OQ-C): if ruled IN, add the additive `ModuleOrigin::Fetched` variant in core (`crates/beamr/src/module.rs:82-89`, `source_atom_name` arm at `crates/beamr/src/module.rs:94-100`, plus match sites) with a native `#[test]` pinning the new variant's atom name; the loader passes `Fetched`. If ruled OUT, the loader passes `Preloaded` and FETCH-MANIFEST.md says so aloud (hazard 12). The `crates/beamr/src/module.rs` entry in this requirement's file list exists only under the IN ruling (ADR-001: the change lives in core).
acceptance: `load_artifacts` is exported on `WasmVm` with the exact `&self` signature and returns a `js_sys::Promise` via `future_to_promise`; the dependency tree is unchanged (no web-sys, no new crates) and `artifact_loader.rs` contains zero `.borrow` calls (grep-provable)

`docs/design/beamr/FETCH-MANIFEST.md` exists with the format table, worked example, the OQ-A-ruled `integrity` disposition, the stated non-goals (no build-time coupling, no modules.bin), and the hazard-7 reload hazard line

Loading follows Kahn order with SCC members in manifest order and cycles reported non-fatally (per the OQ-B ruling; flips with it); `dependency_missing` and `manifest_malformed` reject before any fetch; the post-batch verification pass verifies in-manifest deferred entries healed (registry/export observable, never bucket state) and reports absent targets as `missing_dependencies` data

`ArtifactLoadError` carries `set_name("ArtifactLoadError")` and a `"{kind}: {detail}"` message; the kind-slug set is closed and pinned by a test; decode-stage slugs are produced by matching `LoadError` variants in-crate, never by string-parsing

The success report is a JSON string matching the pinned schema; the `unresolved` entry shape is byte-identical to today's `load_module` report vocabulary; `deferred`/`denied`/`cycles`/`missing_dependencies` are additive

Existing `WasmVm::load_module` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:155-171`) is byte-untouched UNCONDITIONALLY, including its flat-string error at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:163` and its hardcoded `Preloaded` origin at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:161`; if OQ-C rules IN, the `Fetched` origin appears ONLY in `artifact_loader.rs`'s own load calls — never at `:161`

requirements.title: Execution acceptance + boundaries — the end-to-end wall and the byte-untouched surfaces
requirements.spec:
THE END-TO-END WALL (arc acceptance, Node leg): from a true-idle VM, `load_artifacts(manifest_url, fetch_double)` with fixture artifacts loads the chain deps-first, then the host spawns the entry MFA via the EXISTING export surface (`spawn` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:240` / `spawn_actor` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:294` / `send_message` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:174`), real VM execution proceeds under the arbiter, and the wall observes SETTLED completion with the module's result — multi-turn tolerant (`MAX_SLICES_PER_DRAIN = 1_024`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:50`; hazard 18: await settled completion, never one turn), zero manual pump calls.

BOUNDARIES AS DECIDED TEXT (each also a boundaries-array entry): existing exports byte-untouched — `load_module` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:155-171`, flat string `:163`), `send_message` internals with the RATIFIED do-not-reorder comment (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:180-187`; loader-adjacent sends go through the existing method, never replicate its internals — hazard 17), and the completion/summary vocabulary (`summary_to_json`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1559-1578`) unextended so the edge-worker stubs stay untouched (hazard 28). Build-time bundle path untouched (`crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs` in its entirety). NO new wake-path call sites — the complete production inventory, quoted from the ground pack §4 as the closed set the final diff must match: "`schedule_external_edge` defined :430-436 (calls `arbiter.request_external_turn` :433), infallible variant :438-442; production callers: send_message :189, spawn :251, spawn_actor :303, call :325, cast :340, timer_fired :426. Direct `request_external_turn`: arbiter def :585-590, unified-deadline late-fire :1157 (in :1151-1160), promise completion :1292. Test-only sites: :1809, :2050, :3097, :3469, :3478." (file `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs`; line numbers re-verified at the build base per the pin-shift law). NO polling: no recurring callback, no timer whose job is checking whether a fetch finished — completion re-enters via each fetch Promise's own microtask continuation. NO web-sys. Loader failures NEVER route through the arbiter `fail()` latch (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:727-730`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:838-844`) — one bad artifact must not brick the completion surface. H2 (profile law): loader failures surface loader-shaped (`ArtifactLoadError` / report data), never badarg-shaped process errors. JSON-string host-value convention for all loader-emitted values (hazard 5 — no js_sys object payloads outside the Error class itself). 500-line law: both new files stay under 500 code lines. ZERO new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`; `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` untouchable (Vesper's claim).
acceptance: The end-to-end wall passes on the Node leg from true idle: manifest URL in, injected fetch, deps-first load, spawn via existing export, real execution, settled completion with the module's observed result; zero manual pump calls, zero new wake-path call sites

Grep walls hold on the final diff: the wake-path call-site inventory matches the pack §4 closed set (no additions outside tests); zero `web_sys`/`web-sys` tokens; zero `setInterval`/recurring-callback constructs in the loader; zero `arbiter`/`fail(` references in `artifact_loader.rs`; zero `.borrow` in `artifact_loader.rs`
`load_module`, `send_message`, and the completion/summary vocabulary are byte-untouched; `examples/edge-worker` tests pass without stub edits; `crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs` is untouched
No loader failure surfaces as a badarg-shaped process error (H2); every operational failure is an `ArtifactLoadError` rejection and every per-module anomaly is report data
Both new files are under 500 code lines; the final diff carries zero new `#[allow]` and zero non-test `unwrap`/`expect`

requirements.title: Walls + CI + fail-firsts — the thirteen-wall loader battery, the pid-collision rider, exact-33 to exact-N, the authored-not-run browser probe
requirements.spec:
NODE WALLS (own pinned file `artifact_loader_tests.rs`, joining the battery as `connection_events_tests.rs` did; proposed exact names, ratified or amended at the tear): (1) `manifest_chain_loads_dependencies_before_dependants_with_clean_report` — the a-needs-b-needs-c fixture chain, Kahn order observed, report ok with preserved-empty unresolved. (2) `mutual_cycle_pair_loads_in_manifest_order_and_reports_the_scc` — non-fatal, `cycles` entry names both members (flips to a rejection wall if OQ-B rules fatal — one branch + one wall). (3) `dep_edge_naming_module_absent_from_manifest_rejects_dependency_missing` — fatal pre-fetch. (4) `rejecting_fetch_double_rejects_batch_with_artifact_fetch_failed_and_loaded_list` — the rejection carries stage, artifact, and the already-loaded names (OQ-D mechanics pinned here). (5) `malformed_artifact_bytes_reject_with_exact_decode_stage_kind_slugs` — crafted blobs, one per reachable `LoadError` stage, comments naming which variant each must produce. (6) `non_promise_fetch_return_rejects_with_fetch_protocol`. (7) `duplicate_module_names_in_manifest_reject_with_manifest_malformed`. (8) `dependant_before_dependency_defers_then_heals_at_call_time` — the deferred-heals wall: the manifest deliberately OMITS the dep edge and orders the dependant first, so the loader (correctly following manifest order for undeclared edges) loads the dependant before its dependency inside one batch; the dependant's import is Deferred at its load, the post-batch pass verifies it healed once the dependency loads (registry/export observable), and a call through the import succeeds. (9) `post_batch_verification_asserts_in_manifest_deferreds_healed_and_reports_absent_targets`. (10) The R2 end-to-end wall `manifest_url_to_executed_module_end_to_end_reaches_settled_completion`. (11) THE PID-COLLISION RIDER (D13, MUST — Waffles' ruling quoted in the task): `native_and_cooperative_spawns_mint_distinct_pids` — a wasm regression wall proving native-path and cooperative-path spawns mint distinct pids; `alloc_pid` on the shared mint is the sole allocator (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:702`), called from both spawn sites (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:319`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:361`). Placement: beside the existing spawn walls in the lib.rs test region or in `artifact_loader_tests.rs` — builder's pick, recorded; it joins the pinned name list either way. (12) `failing_manifest_fetch_rejects_with_manifest_fetch_failed` — the fetch double rejects on the MANIFEST URL itself; the batch rejects at the manifest stage with `manifest_fetch_failed` and an empty loaded list (the slug's only behavioral wall). (13) `artifact_load_error_kind_slug_set_is_closed_and_exact` — enumerates the closed kind-slug set and asserts exact equality with the eight documented slugs; this is the R1 'pinned by a test' enumeration, counted in the arithmetic.

BORROW WALL: the no-borrow-across-await discipline is enforced as the mechanical grep `grep -n "\.borrow" crates/beamr-wasm/src/artifact_loader.rs` returning empty — expressible, so it rides the workflow's assertion block (else it would fall to the review-bar grep; it does not).

FIXTURES (D10): create `crates/beamr-wasm/fixtures/` — committed `.beam` next to `.erl` sources: the dep chain, the mutual cycle pair, the unresolved-import module, the malformed blob. `include_bytes!` from the wasm test module only; no cross-crate reach into `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures`. First-ness stated in the test-file header: first wasm-side real `.beam` decode executions, first real-VM JS-orchestration tests (pack §6 ground).

CI (D11): `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` — extend the exact-name list AND the exact-count summary from 33 (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-75` names, `:83-88` summary at `03a8474`) to the final N in the SAME COMMIT as the tests, delta-states-cause in the commit message (expected N = 46 with the thirteen names above; any fold- or build-time delta states the delta and its cause in the same commit that moves it). Missing names, stale names, wrong counts, and `no tests to run!` must still fail mechanically. Add the borrow-wall grep to the workflow's assertion block.

FAIL-FIRSTS, recorded-and-reverted per house law, minimum four: (a) invert the Kahn order (dependants first) — walls 1 and 9 red; (b) drop one kind-slug mapping arm — wall 5 red; (c) reintroduce the deferred-bucket drop (report only `unresolved`) — wall 9 red; (d) feed one stale expected name to the workflow assertion — job fails despite passing test output. Run every new pin ten consecutive isolated times.

PROBE DOC (D12): author `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-6-PROBE-FETCH.md` as part of this brief — real `fetch()` via the one-line adapter (`(url) => fetch(url).then(r => { if (!r.ok) throw new Error(String(r.status)); return r.arrayBuffer(); })`), real HTTP server, real manifest URL, expected observations enumerated; AUTHORED, NOT RUN — the run is gated on Tom's/Annabel's word, observations never CI acceptance (WPORT-3 probe precedent, `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-3-PROBE-THROTTLE.md:1-28`). Ops note carried: may share a browser sitting with the still-OPEN WPORT-3 deadline probe; sequencing unowned by this brief.
acceptance: All thirteen Node walls pass under the pinned wasm-bindgen runner from true idle, ten consecutive isolated runs each; no wall asserts timing precision; no wall drives progress with a manual pump
The pid-collision rider proves a native-path spawn and a cooperative-path spawn in one VM mint distinct pids, discharging Waffles' arc :113 line item; its name joins the pinned list and count

`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` names every final wasm test exactly and asserts the exact final summary count in the same commit as the tests, with the delta and its cause stated in the commit message; the 33-name baseline remains green and unweakened; the borrow-wall grep rides the workflow
`crates/beamr-wasm/fixtures/` exists with `.beam` committed beside `.erl` sources and a comment on the malformed blob naming its intended `LoadError`; no cross-crate fixture includes
Four fail-first mutations are performed, captured in the handoff, and reverted
`docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-6-PROBE-FETCH.md` exists, authored-not-run, with the fetch adapter line, the serving recipe, and the expected observations; nothing in CI depends on it

boundaries: ADDITIVE-ONLY SURFACE: every existing `WasmVm` export is byte-untouched — `load_module` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:155-171`) keeps its flat-string error (`:163`) and its report shape; retrofitting structured errors onto it is OUT of scope. `send_message` internals with the RATIFIED do-not-reorder comment (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:180-187`) are never replicated (hazard 17). The completion/summary vocabulary (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1559-1578`) is NOT extended; edge-worker stubs stay untouched (hazard 28).

OUT OF SCOPE: bytecode-orchestrated loading — RULED OUT with recorded reason (settled-idle/awaitExit evidence, ground pack §8.2, cited in the purpose block); no settled-idle extension, no outstanding-completion count, no guest-visible fetch BIF is built here. The port-design fetch-seam ruling (`docs/WASM-RUNTIME-PORT-DESIGN.md:158-161`) is respected by construction: the loader exposes nothing to bytecode.

OUT OF SCOPE: the build-time bundle path — `crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs` untouched in its entirety; the `beamr-wasm-bundle` manifest is not extended; `modules.bin` is not a fetch target; `BEAMR_WASM_BUNDLE_DIR` never enters the runtime path.

OUT OF SCOPE (D14): wiring a cooperative code-management facility — `code:all_loaded/0` keeps its badarg-refusal per the WPORT-5 'registration must not imply support' discipline; the six sealed profile rows' documented classes do not move.

OUT OF SCOPE (D7/D9): healing a permanent Unresolved without reloading the dependant (nothing in the bytes supports it); cross-batch dedupe or reload guards (clobber-with-generation+1 is the documented semantic); integrity ENFORCEMENT (OQ-A reserves the field at most).

PROHIBITION inherited from WPORT-4/5 verbatim: no new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site outside tests; the pack §4 inventory quoted in R2 is the closed set; an apparent need for a new site is a STOP-and-report deviation. NO-POLLING binds: no recurring check anywhere in the loader; fetch completion re-enters via each Promise's own microtask continuation.
NO web-sys, no new dependencies; the wasm closure stays `cooperative,json` (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:13-36`) and both locked `cargo check` closures stay green.

FAIL-LATCH EXCLUSION: loader failures reject the `load_artifacts` Promise only; the arbiter `fail()` latch (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:727-730`, `:838-844`) is never invoked by the loader — one bad artifact must not brick `await_exit` for the VM.

H2 (profile law): loader failures are loader-shaped — `ArtifactLoadError` or report data — never badarg-shaped process errors. House JSON-string convention for all loader-emitted host values (hazard 5); the two structured-error precedents (thrown typed error vs reasons-vocabulary data) are not hybridized.

BORROW DISCIPLINE: zero `.borrow` calls in `artifact_loader.rs` — the loader future holds `Arc` clones only ([re-verified at 03a8474]: `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:50-62`); no RefCell borrow can therefore be held across an await point. Re-entrancy: `load_artifacts` from an in-drain callback is the same documented no as every ingress (hazard 16).

Untouchable: `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` (Vesper's claim). 500-code-line law on both new files. ZERO new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]`. No builds in temporary directories, ever.

open_questions.question: Does manifest v1 reserve an optional per-module `integrity` field (reserved-but-unenforced)?
open_questions.recommendation: YES — reserve the field name now in the v1 schema and FETCH-MANIFEST.md; enforcement is explicitly out of scope. Prevents a v2 format bump for the obvious next ask. Cost: one doc row and one parser-ignores-it line.

open_questions.question: Is a dependency cycle in the manifest fatal, or a non-fatal structured diagnosis? The arc names 'cycles or unsatisfied ordering' among the structured-failure producers (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:134` at `03a8474`), but strict topo is impossible within an SCC and Deferred heals at call time (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/core.rs:561-599`; no cycle machinery exists or is needed, pack §3).
open_questions.recommendation: NON-FATAL, reported: SCC members load in manifest order and the SCC is a structured, named `cycles` report entry — the arc clause satisfied as a structured diagnosis. Mutual recursion is legal BEAM reality; refusing it would reject working artifact sets. If the tear reads the arc clause as refusal, the flip costs one branch and one wall (R3 wall 2).

open_questions.question: Is the additive `ModuleOrigin::Fetched` core variant in scope (ADR-001: the change lives in core — `crates/beamr/src/module.rs:82-89`, `source_atom_name` at `:94-100`, match sites), or does the loader pass `Preloaded`?
open_questions.recommendation: IN — one enum variant, one `source_atom_name` arm, the match sites, and a native pin test for the atom name. Honest provenance beats hardcoded `Preloaded` (hazard 12). If ruled OUT, the loader passes `Preloaded` and FETCH-MANIFEST.md says so aloud; the `crates/beamr/src/module.rs` file entry in R1 drops.

open_questions.question: Rejection-payload mechanics: individual properties on the `ArtifactLoadError` object, or one JSON `data` field?
open_questions.recommendation: Drafter's pick, made concrete for the tear to ratify: ONE `data` property on the Error object holding a JSON STRING `{"artifact":<name>,"url":<url>,"stage":<kind slug>,"loaded":[<names loaded before failure>]}` — a single `js_sys::Reflect::set`, and it follows the house JSON-string host-value convention (hazard 5) instead of minting a js-object hybrid. Test-pinned in R3 wall 4.

verification:
BUILD-LEG ORIENTATION carries Tom's standing rule verbatim: "NO builds in temporary directories, ever." Worktrees go under the repo (`<repo>/.worktrees/<name>`, `.worktrees/` in `.git/info/exclude` — already added for beamr), every build sets `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=<repo>/target`, logs go to the artifacts dir.

FIRST ACTION at the build head — the PIN-SHIFT RE-VERIFY (believed state is not citable state; every citation in this brief is at `03a8474` and main may have moved): re-verify at the ACTUAL base commit and record the re-verified line numbers in the handoff: (1) the load seam (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:155-171`, origin `:161`, flat-string `:163`, report `:164-170`); (2) the wake-path call-site inventory (pack §4 closed set — `schedule_external_edge` def/callers and the three direct `request_external_turn` sites); (3) the `WasmVm` shared-handle shape (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:50-62`); (4) the core report accessors (`crates/beamr/src/loader/load.rs:157-164`, `:183-190`, `:203-210`); (5) the pid mint (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:702` sole allocator; spawn sites `:319`/`:361`); (6) the CI baseline (exactly 33 names, `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-75`, summary `:83-88`); (7) `LoadError` variants (`crates/beamr/src/error.rs:16-30`) and the `ConnectionEventProtocolError` pattern (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/connection_events.rs:515-519`). Any drift: re-derive, restate in the handoff, and proceed only if the design premises hold — else STOP condition 1 applies.

THE 9-LEG GATE BAR on the final head (as WPORT-5's brief carries it): (1) `cargo fmt --check`; (2)+(3) fresh-lint both clippy legs per the amended gate law — `cargo clean -p <crate>` before `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, Compiling/Checking line captured beside the exit code; cached greens stamped "checksum-verified cache hit", never "fresh re-lint"; (4) `cargo test --workspace --all-features --lib`; (5) `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'`; (6) `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker` (must pass WITHOUT stub edits — the vocabulary boundary held); (7) `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p beamr --no-default-features --features cooperative,json --locked`; (8) `cargo check --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked`; (9) the pinned Node leg `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture`, requiring the UPDATED workflow assertion to find every exact name and the exact final summary count (33 + the new pins; expected 46), with missing names, stale names, wrong counts, and `no tests to run!` still failing — verified against the actual run output, not the yml alone.

PROHIBITION GREPS ride the bar: the final diff adds no `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site outside tests (inventory match against the pack §4 closed set); zero `web_sys` tokens; zero new `#[allow]`; zero non-test `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`; zero `.borrow` in `artifact_loader.rs`; zero `setInterval`/recurring-callback constructs and zero arbiter-`fail()` references in the loader; `crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs` and `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` untouched.

Run every new pin in isolation ten consecutive times. Perform, record, and REVERT the four R3 fail-first mutations.

Stamp the handoff with final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose`, `cargo --version`, `wasm-bindgen-test-runner --version`, `node --version`, wasm target, UTC timestamps, exact commands, the exact final test inventory, fail-first outputs, and the pin-shift re-verify table. The handoff CARRIES: the FETCH-MANIFEST.md path and schema; the closed kind-slug set; the pinned success-report schema; the OQ-D payload shape as ratified; the probe doc path with its authored-not-run status and gating word; and the arc-board lines — the pid-collision rider discharged by the R3 wall (arc `:113` line item), and the still-open WPORT-3 deadline-pillar browser+Worker probe line, terms unchanged.
